title: bellevalia montana
intro: Bellevalia montana, also known as alpine squill, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. It is a perennial bulbous plant, native to the mountainous regions of Europe, known for its clusters of blue or white bell-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its expectorant and diuretic properties, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation. Bellevalia montana can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as colds, fever, and digestive issues, offering potential health benefits including reduced inflammation and improved immune function.
